title: Time-to-Hire and Recruiting Pipeline Health
dataSources: Primary: `index=recruiting` carrying ATS-sourced sourcetypes defined locally because no Splunkbase TA canonicalizes applicant-tracking pipelines end-to-end. Typical combinations: `dbx:ats_requisitions`, `dbx:ats_applications`, `dbx:ats_stages` from Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) JDBC extracts against Snowflake, BigQuery, or SQL Server warehouses fed by Greenhouse, Lever, iCIMS, or ELT tools; `greenhouse:job` / `greenhouse:application` from Harvest REST modular inputs; `lever:opportunity` / `lever:posting` from Lever REST pollers; `workday:rec_requisition` when Workday Recruiting exposes Report-as-a-Service JSON or SOAP job-posting extracts. Minimum viable attributes per req row: logical `req_key`, opening timestamps (`opened_*`, `posted_*`), closure timestamps (`filled_*`, `closed_*`), recruiter ownership, department or hiring-team slice, lifecycle `status`, structured `close_reason`, optional `crit_priority` via lookup from department or req template to escalation tiers. Candidate-provided emails or legal names belong off dashboards — strip via defensive `fields -` removal plus index-time `SEDCMD` transforms.
app: Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) plus bespoke modular-input apps wrapping ATS REST, Workday RaaS, or Workday Web Services SOAP endpoints — parallel to UC-23.4.1’s observation that Workday lacks a turnkey Splunk TA, except recruiting dimensions replace Worker-centric facts.
spl:
index=recruiting (sourcetype="dbx:ats_requisitions" OR sourcetype="greenhouse:job" OR sourcetype="lever:posting" OR sourcetype="workday:rec_requisition") earliest=-180d@d latest=now
| fields - candidate_email candidate_phone candidate_name
| eval req_key=coalesce(req_id,requisition_id,Job_Requisition_ID,opening_id,posting_id,"")
| eval opened_raw=coalesce(opened_date,opened_at,posted_date,Posted_Date,openedAt,"")
| eval filled_raw=coalesce(filled_date,closed_date,Filled_Date,hire_date,hiredAt,"")
| eval dept=coalesce(department,Department,dept,hiring_team,"")
| eval recruiter_field=coalesce(recruiter,Recruiter,recruiter_name,owner_name,"")
| eval opened_epoch=case(match(opened_raw,"^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[^Z]+[+-][0-9]{4}$"),strptime(opened_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"),like(opened_raw,"%Z"),strptime(replace(opened_raw,"Z",""),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),like(opened_raw,"% %:%:%"),strptime(opened_raw,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),1=1,strptime(opened_raw,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval filled_epoch=case(match(filled_raw,"^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[^Z]+[+-][0-9]{4}$"),strptime(filled_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"),like(filled_raw,"%Z"),strptime(replace(filled_raw,"Z",""),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),like(filled_raw,"% %:%:%"),strptime(filled_raw,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),1=1,strptime(filled_raw,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval hire_marker=case(lower(status) IN ("closed - filled","filled","filled - hired"),1,lower(close_reason) IN ("hire","filled","hired"),1,match(lower(tostring(close_reason)),"(filled|hire)"),1,1=1,0)
| eval withdrawn=case(lower(status) IN ("closed - cancelled","cancelled","closed - not hired","on hold closed"),1,match(lower(tostring(close_reason)),"(cancel|withdraw|freeze)"),1,1=1,0)
| eval pipeline_status=case(hire_marker=1 AND isnotnull(filled_epoch),"Filled",withdrawn=1,"Withdrawn",1=1,"Open")
| eval age_days=if(isnotnull(opened_epoch),round((now()-opened_epoch)/86400,0),null())
| eval staleness_bucket=case(age_days>180,"Critical (>180d)",age_days>120,"Severe (>120d)",age_days>90,"Stale (>90d)",age_days>60,"Aging (60-90d)",age_days>30,"Maturing (30-60d)",isnotnull(age_days),"Active (<30d)",1=1,"Unknown")
| lookup recruiting_critical_roles dept OUTPUT crit_priority
| eval crit_priority=coalesce(crit_priority,"Standard")
| stats avg(eval(if(pipeline_status="Filled",(filled_epoch-opened_epoch)/86400,null()))) as avg_dtf median(eval(if(pipeline_status="Filled",(filled_epoch-opened_epoch)/86400,null()))) as med_dtf dc(eval(if(pipeline_status="Filled",req_key,null()))) as filled dc(eval(if(pipeline_status="Open" AND match(staleness_bucket,"Stale|Severe|Critical"),req_key,null()))) as stale_hot dc(req_key) as total_reqs by dept, recruiter_field
| eval fill_rate=round(100*filled/nullif(total_reqs,0),1)
| sort - stale_hot
| table dept, recruiter_field, total_reqs, filled, fill_rate, stale_hot, avg_dtf, med_dtf
description: Measures recruiting funnel health on ATS feeds by stitching requisition lifecycle signals — opened versus posted versus screening versus interview versus offer versus hire — computing multi-tier staleness buckets for aging reqs, deriving department-by-recruiter fill-rate tables, and exposing bottleneck hotspots where reqs linger without advancing.
value: Hiring velocity signals whether product and revenue organizations can scale capacity behind booked plans; stretched weeks-to-fill on engineering or quota-bearing roles translates into roadmap slips and bookings risk quarters later. Heatmapping recruiter-by-department intersections reveals hiring-manager stalls versus recruiter sourcing gaps sooner than blended enterprise averages mute localized dysfunction.
implementation: (1) Establish ingestion across whichever ATS path applies — warehouse JDBC (`dbx:ats_*`), Harvest or Lever REST modular inputs, or Workday Recruiting RaaS or SOAP — codifying sourcetypes plus redaction rules before dashboards touch indexes. (2) Publish lookup `recruiting_critical_roles` aligning departments or req templates with escalation tiers (`Critical`, `High`, `Standard`). (3) Schedule baseline KPI searches weekly with timezone-aware cron while Critical-role alerts execute daily using narrower windows and throttle keys on `req_key`. (4) Pair Splunk tiles with ATS-native deeplink macros rather than embedding candidate identifiers inside alert emails. (5) Refresh Splunk-side taxonomy filters quarterly whenever ATS workflow definitions rename hiring freeze or withdrawn statuses.
visualization: (1) Single-value tiles for ninety-day median days-to-fill and count of reqs breaching Severe staleness tiers. (2) Heatmap or pivot matrix with departments on one axis and funnel stage or staleness bucket on the other, colored by average dwell duration. (3) Sortable table for recruiter × department intersections surfacing fill-rate versus stale backlog — prioritize combos where stale_hot climbs while fill_rate deteriorates. (4) Companion line chart for trailing-twelve-month hiring velocity measured as fills per calendar month. (5) Secondary ribbon comparing withdrawn reqs versus fills so finance interprets leakage distinctly from throughput.

references.title: Workday — Talent Management Recruiting product overview

knownFalsePositives: Corporate hiring freezes inflate naive days-open because ATS clocks tick while sourcing idles — overlay finance freeze calendars before blaming TA throughput. Reopened reqs that recycle identifiers after Cancelled–Not-Filled closures distort longitudinal fill-rate unless reopen timestamps split lifetimes across closures. Executive searches spanning twelve-plus months skew department averages — isolate VP-plus cohorts so median KPI panels exclude deliberate slow slate-building. Multi-headcount openings modeled as one parent req versus multiple child openings scramble denominators — reconcile ATS quota semantics before comparing recruiters. Large Workday RaaS prompts sometimes truncate silently — chart uniqueness hourly alongside ingestion alarms whenever Splunk totals diverge materially from warehouse totals. Harvest repeat applicants inflate application-centric widgets versus candidate-centric funnel breadth — caption dashboards with which cardinality each tile counts. Warehouse UTC timestamps versus recruiter-local UI assumptions inject boundary ghosts — publish timezone SPL macros shared across TA analytics apps. Ghost ownership after recruiter exits keeps reqs Open despite dormant pipelines — HR hygiene precedes escalation. Acquisition migrations stamping legacy backlog as opened today create spikes — suppress governance-tagged synthetic migrations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Dedicated Splunk indexes `recruiting` (365-day retention for trailing hiring velocity) and optionally `summary_recruiting` for accelerated KPI snapshots; roles `recruiting_analyst` with `srchIndexesAllowed = recruiting; summary_recruiting` and least-privilege dashboards — candidate-contact fields never appear on broad executive tiles.
• Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) on a Heavy Forwarder with JDBC reachability to the ATS warehouse **or** outbound HTTPS `443/tcp` from a modular-input host to Harvest/Lever/Workday endpoints plus Splunk HTTP Event Collector `8088/tcp` to indexers or Splunk Cloud.
• API secrets stored outside indexes (either Credential Storage in DB Connect or modular-input encrypted credential fields); Integration credentials rotated quarterly.
• Field glossary documented in Confluence: map each ATS vendor column to logical names (`req_key`, `opened_epoch`, `filled_epoch`, `close_reason`, `status`) because **there is no canonical Splunkbase TA** that normalizes Greenhouse, Lever, Workday Recruiting, iCIMS, SmartRecruiters, Jobvite, BambooHR, or Taleo — sourcetypes such as `dbx:ats_requisitions`, `greenhouse:job`, `lever:posting`, `workday:rec_requisition` are deployment conventions defined in `props.conf`, analogous to UC-23.4.1’s Workday HCM patterns but targeting recruiting facts rather than Worker lifecycle dimensions.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

Path A — Greenhouse Harvest REST (polling modular input): In Greenhouse → Configure → Dev Center create an API key (minimum scopes for Jobs + Applications). Configure a Python modular input or Splunk Add-on Builder HTTP task that performs paginated GET calls to `https://harvest.greenhouse.io/v1/jobs` and `https://harvest.greenhouse.io/v1/applications`, honoring `Link` / `X-Greenhouse-Pagination` continuation headers until pages exhaust. Sink newline-delimited JSON to HEC with `sourcetype=greenhouse:job` / `greenhouse:application` and `index=recruiting`. Example `inputs.conf` stanza skeleton:

```
[python_modular_input://greenhouse_harvest_jobs]
python.version = python3
interval = 3600
param.base_url = https://harvest.greenhouse.io/v1/jobs
param.hec_uri = https://<splunk-hec>:8088/services/collector/event
param.hec_token = <token_ref>
index = recruiting
sourcetype = greenhouse:job
```

Ensure application payloads exclude prospect emails from analytic summaries — strip at ingestion before HEC send.

Path B — DB Connect JDBC into an ATS-backed warehouse: Install `splunk_app_db_connect`, define Connection `ATS_DW_JDBC` (Snowflake/BigQuery/SQL Server depending on export). Create `db_input` SQL akin to:

```
SELECT req_id, department, recruiter_name, opened_date, filled_date, close_reason, status, last_updated FROM mart.recruiting_requisitions WHERE last_updated > ?
```

Bind `last_updated` as a rising watermark (`2025-01-01 00:00:00` bootstrap). Set output sourcetype `dbx:ats_requisitions`, index `recruiting`, interval 15–60 minutes. TIMESTAMP columns typically arrive as `%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S` UTC unless the warehouse preserves tenant TZ — note that in props.

Path C — Workday Recruiting via Report-as-a-Service: Publish custom report `INT_REC_OpenRequisitions` exposing `Job_Requisition_ID`, `Title`, `Department`, `Posted_Date`, `Filled_Date`, `Stage`, `Recruiter`, `Close_Reason`. Enable web-service URL similar to:

`https://wd5-services1.workday.com/ccx/service/customreport2/<tenant>/<owner>/INT_REC_OpenRequisitions?format=json`

Poll hourly using Integration System User credentials from a modular input; POST JSON lines to HEC with `sourcetype=workday:rec_requisition`. Date strings commonly appear ISO-8601 with `%z`; extremely wide tenants must partition prompts by calendar quarter so each execution stays below timeout thresholds discussed in troubleshooting.

Index-time minimization and hygiene: add `props.conf` on first parsing tier:

```
[greenhouse:job]
SEDCMD-redact_email = s/[\w.+-]+@[\w.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}/[REDACTED_EMAIL]/g

[dbx:ats_requisitions]
SEDCMD-redact_phone = s/\b\d{3}-\d{3}-\d{4}\b/[REDACTED_PHONE]/g
```

Pair with transforms-based routing so recruiting indexes never inherit unrelated HRIS snapshots. Maintain summary index `recruiting_kpi_summary` populated nightly by scheduled summary-generation jobs — reduces dashboard scan load while preserving twelve-month hiring velocity curves.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

Operational SPL (multi-dialect field normalization — paste into Search; rename index only if your governance maps reqs under `business` instead of `recruiting`):

```spl
index=recruiting (sourcetype="dbx:ats_requisitions" OR sourcetype="greenhouse:job" OR sourcetype="lever:posting" OR sourcetype="workday:rec_requisition") earliest=-180d@d latest=now
| fields - candidate_email candidate_phone candidate_name
| eval req_key=coalesce(req_id,requisition_id,Job_Requisition_ID,opening_id,posting_id,"")
| eval opened_raw=coalesce(opened_date,opened_at,posted_date,Posted_Date,openedAt,"")
| eval filled_raw=coalesce(filled_date,closed_date,Filled_Date,hire_date,hiredAt,"")
| eval dept=coalesce(department,Department,dept,hiring_team,"")
| eval recruiter_field=coalesce(recruiter,Recruiter,recruiter_name,owner_name,"")
| eval opened_epoch=case(match(opened_raw,"^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[^Z]+[+-][0-9]{4}$"),strptime(opened_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"),like(opened_raw,"%Z"),strptime(replace(opened_raw,"Z",""),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),like(opened_raw,"% %:%:%"),strptime(opened_raw,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),1=1,strptime(opened_raw,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval filled_epoch=case(match(filled_raw,"^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[^Z]+[+-][0-9]{4}$"),strptime(filled_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"),like(filled_raw,"%Z"),strptime(replace(filled_raw,"Z",""),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),like(filled_raw,"% %:%:%"),strptime(filled_raw,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),1=1,strptime(filled_raw,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval hire_marker=case(lower(status) IN ("closed - filled","filled","filled - hired"),1,lower(close_reason) IN ("hire","filled","hired"),1,match(lower(tostring(close_reason)),"(filled|hire)"),1,1=1,0)
| eval withdrawn=case(lower(status) IN ("closed - cancelled","cancelled","closed - not hired","on hold closed"),1,match(lower(tostring(close_reason)),"(cancel|withdraw|freeze)"),1,1=1,0)
| eval pipeline_status=case(hire_marker=1 AND isnotnull(filled_epoch),"Filled",withdrawn=1,"Withdrawn",1=1,"Open")
| eval age_days=if(isnotnull(opened_epoch),round((now()-opened_epoch)/86400,0),null())
| eval staleness_bucket=case(age_days>180,"Critical (>180d)",age_days>120,"Severe (>120d)",age_days>90,"Stale (>90d)",age_days>60,"Aging (60-90d)",age_days>30,"Maturing (30-60d)",isnotnull(age_days),"Active (<30d)",1=1,"Unknown")
| lookup recruiting_critical_roles dept OUTPUT crit_priority
| eval crit_priority=coalesce(crit_priority,"Standard")
| stats avg(eval(if(pipeline_status="Filled",(filled_epoch-opened_epoch)/86400,null()))) as avg_dtf median(eval(if(pipeline_status="Filled",(filled_epoch-opened_epoch)/86400,null()))) as med_dtf dc(eval(if(pipeline_status="Filled",req_key,null()))) as filled dc(eval(if(pipeline_status="Open" AND match(staleness_bucket,"Stale|Severe|Critical"),req_key,null()))) as stale_hot dc(req_key) as total_reqs by dept, recruiter_field
| eval fill_rate=round(100*filled/nullif(total_reqs,0),1)
| sort - stale_hot
| table dept, recruiter_field, total_reqs, filled, fill_rate, stale_hot, avg_dtf, med_dtf
```

Branch logic covers ISO timestamps carrying offsets from Workday RaaS, JDBC TIMESTAMP strings without TZ (interpretation inherits warehouse convention), and Harvest UTC timestamps ending with `Z`.

Schedule the managerial dashboard weekly Monday `06:00` customer timezone (`cron 0 6 * * 1`). Schedule Critical escalation searches daily `04:00`, filtering rows where `crit_priority` equals Critical from lookup results, `pipeline_status` stays Open, and `age_days` exceeds sixty — alert recipients include hiring manager routing lists plus TA leadership — throttle keys on `req_key` seventy-two hours.

Stage bottleneck companion SPL when stage-history feeds exist (`dbx:ats_stages` or vendor-native exports):

```spl
index=recruiting sourcetype="dbx:ats_stages" earliest=-90d
| eval req_key=coalesce(req_id,requisition_id,Job_Requisition_ID,"")
| eval dwell_hours=(strptime(stage_exit_ts,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")-strptime(stage_enter_ts,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/3600
| stats avg(dwell_hours) as avg_hours_in_stage by stage_name, dept
```

Never blend those averages directly into headline velocity KPIs — executives interpret blended charts as throughput changes when only interviewing slows.

**Step 3 — Validate**

Pick ten closed reqs spanning fast fills under thirty days and slow fills beyond ninety days. Compare Splunk-derived intervals against each ATS dashboard UI — Greenhouse Reports → Time-to-Fill; Lever Insights → Speed; Workday Recruiting Hub analytics — noting intentional definition mismatches (`posted_date` versus `approved_date`, inclusive versus exclusive boundaries).

Spot-check recruiter ownership by drilling matching reqs inside ATS UI — mismatched recruiter_field versus embedded hiring-team delegates signals stale delegation metadata worth correcting upstream.

Stage-history reconciliation uses ATS-native timelines — Greenhouse Activity Feed versus Workday Process History — verifying Splunk dwell-hours panels mirror recruiter anecdotes.

Embed documented numerator/denominator language directly inside dashboard subtitles so audit comparisons reuse identical semantics quarter-over-quarter.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**

Dashboard rows: headline single-value median days-to-fill for ninety-day cohort of filled reqs; heatmap crossing department with funnel stage heat via companion search; stale-req drill table carrying ATS deeplink macro sans candidate identifiers; monthly hiring velocity trendline.

Optional overlay counting overdue interviewer-feedback tasks per hiring manager sourced from ATS tasks API feeds — exposes hiring-manager bottleneck separately from recruiter sourcing cadence.

Operational rhythm: weekly TA leadership forum referencing Splunk intersections; monthly TA-plus-business-unit forums interpreting recruiter × department tiles; quarterly threshold tuning referencing workforce-planning forecasts.

Accountable executive owner remains Head of Talent Acquisition with deputy escalation documented inside HR ticketing taxonomy.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

• **Withdrawn closures miscounted as hires —** Both Greenhouse and Lever stamp `closed_at` irrespective of Cancelled versus Hire semantics — naive `isnotnull(filled_date)` treats withdrawals as successes. Discovery SPL for taxonomy hygiene: `index=recruiting sourcetype IN ("greenhouse:job","lever:posting") earliest=-90d | stats count by status, close_reason | sort - count`. Align Splunk filters with ATS-native enumerations (`Closed - Filled`, `Closed - Cancelled`) before computing fill-rate KPIs; publish withdrawn totals separately.

• **Workday RaaS timeouts truncate breadth —** Tenants exceeding tens of thousands of rows per unprompted execution frequently observe HTTP timeouts (~300 seconds); modular inputs might ingest trailing-most slices repeatedly while uniqueness metrics flatten. Inspect `_internal` ExecProcessor traces tied to your integration worker plus hourly `dc(Job_Requisition_ID)` trending — stagnation triggers mitigation: tighten calendar-quarter prompts, adopt SOAP pagination (`Response_Filter` Page / Count_Per_Page), or split prompts via Studio filters.

• **`application_id` versus `candidate_id` divergence —** Five applications from one prospect inflate throughput widgets differently than diversity widgets — diagnostic SPL: `index=recruiting sourcetype="greenhouse:application" earliest=-90d | stats dc(application_id) as apps dc(candidate_id) as humans eval(round(apps/humans,2)) as apps_per_human by dept`. Ratios materially above two advise splitting KPI tiles — throughput versus funnel breadth — so executives resolve contradictory narratives during quarterly reviews.

• **Timezone drift across warehouse extracts versus Splunk indexing —** JDBC TIMESTAMP interpreted as UTC while Ops assumes Eastern yields silent ±one-day swings near quarter boundaries — centralize macros emitting consistent TZ-adjusted fields prior to SLA comparisons.

• **Ghost reqs lingering after recruiter transitions —** Ownership stale yet req stays Open exaggerates staleness buckets absent hygiene passes tying recruiter_field refresh cadence to HRIS termination signals.

• **Duplicate ATS identifiers following acquisitions —** Imported backlog stamped as newly opened creates artificial spikes — governance tags marking migrated backlog suppress executive panic until validated against ATS provenance feeds.
grandmaExplanation: We watch each open job crawl through recruiting stages, count how many weeks until someone accepts, and point out hiring-manager bottlenecks before backlog slows launches everyone counted on.
